title: quercus libani var. eigii
intro: Quercus libani var. eigii, also known as the Lebanese oak, is a rare and endangered tree species native to the Mediterranean region. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus libani var. eigii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of the tree's bark and leaves can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
